About Me

Hi, I am Xiting Zhuang (庄曦庭). I’m currently a Research Assistant Professor in the Department of Agribusiness and Applied Economics at North Dakota State University.

My research focuses on global supply chain disruptions, agri-food trade, and sustainability, with a particular interest in how evolving international policies and economic shocks shape global food systems. By incorporating big data analytics and machine learning techniques, I aim to uncover insights that inform resilient and sustainable trade strategies.

This site serves as a platform to share my research, professional experiences, and collaborations. If you are interested in my work or exploring opportunities for collaboration, feel free to reach out to me at xiting.zhuang@ndsu.edu.

📄 View My CV

Research Interests

  • Causal Inference
  • International Trade
  • Supply Chain
  • Environmental Economics

Latest News

  • Feb 2026: Paper accepted: ‘Machine learning-assisted abstract screening on learning analytics: A step-by-step tutorial’ in Systematic Reviews.
  • Jan 2025: New publication: ‘Parental educational pairings and child physical abuse: Evidence from China’ in International Journal of Educational Development.
  • Jan 2025: New publication: ‘Work environment and intimate partner violence against women: Evidence from China’ in Applied Economics.
  • Jan 2025: New publication: ‘The 2021 container shipping crisis and its consequences for US agricultural exports’ in Journal of Transport Economics and Policy.
  • Jan 2025: New publication: ‘Trade implications of ending China’s zero-Covid policy’ in Asian Economics Letters.
